cards.headline: Hormuz shipping falls sharply after new Iran strikes
cards.summary: Data shows a significant decline in ships — many carrying oil and gas — transiting the Strait of Hormuz following attacks this week. Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky global economy. Gas prices have also fluctuated for U.S. consumers since the U.S. and Israel launched operations against Iran, disrupting shipping through the waterway.

quiz.prompt: What happened to crude oil prices after Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran?
quiz.explanation: According to the snippets, crude oil prices jumped after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding uncertainty to the global economy.

cards.headline: Used EV prices surge 12% amid Iran conflict
cards.summary: The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index for EVs increased 12% compared to June 2025, as high gas prices tied to the Iran conflict drive demand. The spike comes as Iran-related disruptions push consumers toward electric vehicles. Used EVs keep getting more expensive as the broader conflict adds upward pressure on energy and auto markets.

quiz.prompt: By how much did the Manheim Used EV Value Index increase compared to June 2025?
quiz.explanation: The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index for EVs increased 12% compared to June 2025, driven by high gas prices linked to the Iran conflict.

cards.headline: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive feeds
cards.summary: EU regulators have threatened Meta with fines, saying features such as infinite scroll on Facebook and Instagram contribute to 'compulsive use' and 'unhealthy habits.' The move signals an escalation in European oversight of social media platforms under digital rules. Meta has not yet responded publicly to the regulatory findings.

quiz.prompt: Which specific design features did EU regulators cite as contributing to compulsive use on Meta's platforms?
quiz.explanation: EU regulators specifically cited features such as infinite scroll as contributing to compulsive use and unhealthy habits on Facebook and Instagram.

cards.headline: SK Hynix raises $26.5bn in record Nasdaq debut
cards.summary: Chip giant SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in what is described as a record-breaking foreign listing on the Nasdaq. Shares surged as much as 17% in their trading debut on Friday. The bumper listing reflects strong investor appetite for semiconductor companies tied to the AI boom.

quiz.prompt: How much did SK Hynix shares surge during their Nasdaq trading debut?
quiz.explanation: SK Hynix shares surged as much as 17% in their Nasdaq trading debut, making it a record-breaking foreign listing.

cards.headline: US home sales drop as prices hit all-time high
cards.summary: Home sales fell in June month over month as mortgage rates remain stubbornly high, according to new data. Despite weaker sales, prices reached an all-time high. Separately, real estate agents are increasingly reporting a more balanced market, with the share reporting price cuts on active listings dropping dramatically in recent surveys.

quiz.prompt: What happened to US home prices in June despite weaker sales?
options: They fell to a two-year low
They rose modestly below prior records
quiz.explanation: Home sales dropped in June as mortgage rates stayed high, but prices still reached an all-time high according to the data cited.

cards.headline: Delta expects higher airfares to last into 2026
cards.summary: Delta Air Lines reported a $1.4 billion profit despite its highest quarterly fuel expense in history, according to Guardian and CNBC reports. CEO comments indicate elevated airfares are expected to last, bringing the airline's 2026 profit goal within reach. Delta is the first major U.S. airline to report second-quarter results, setting the tone for the sector.

quiz.prompt: What did Delta report alongside its expectation that higher airfares will last?
options: Its lowest quarterly fuel expense ever
quiz.explanation: Delta reported a $1.4 billion profit despite its highest quarterly fuel expense in history, while its CEO said elevated airfares are likely to last.

cards.headline: VW workers protest sweeping job cuts and closures
cards.summary: Volkswagen's proposal to slash up to 100,000 jobs and close factories faced a major test as workers staged demonstrations at 18 sites across Germany. The protests came as the radical transformation plan was put to the board of Europe's biggest carmaker. The scale of the proposed cuts has alarmed unions and employees across VW's German manufacturing base.

quiz.prompt: At how many sites did Volkswagen workers stage protests over proposed job cuts?
quiz.explanation: Workers demonstrated at 18 sites across Germany in response to Volkswagen's proposal to slash up to 100,000 jobs and close factories.

cards.headline: Rivian stock drops 18% after $75m share sale
cards.summary: Rivian's stock fell 18% after the company sold 75 million shares to raise capital in extended hours trading. The share sale followed a strong run for the stock, which had risen 8.1% on Monday after surging 19.2% the prior week. The dilutive capital raise signals Rivian still needs significant funding as it scales production.

quiz.prompt: By how much did Rivian's stock fall after it announced a sale of 75 million shares?
quiz.explanation: Rivian stock fell 18% after the company sold 75 million shares to raise capital during extended hours trading.

cards.headline: Toyota invests $3.6bn to shift Tacoma production to Texas
cards.summary: Toyota announced a $3.6 billion investment to move production of its Tacoma midsize pickup truck from Mexico to its San Antonio manufacturing campus in Texas. The move is one of the largest manufacturing investment announcements in the auto sector amid ongoing tariff pressures. It signals a broader trend of automakers reshoring production to the United States.

quiz.prompt: Where is Toyota moving Tacoma pickup truck production from Mexico to?
quiz.explanation: Toyota said it is investing $3.6 billion to move Tacoma production from Mexico to its San Antonio manufacturing campus in Texas.

cards.headline: PepsiCo misses earnings as US consumers pull back
cards.summary: PepsiCo reported weaker-than-expected quarterly earnings, missing analyst estimates as U.S. consumers tighten their budgets. International demand remained strong, partly offsetting domestic weakness. The results add to a growing picture of pressure on American consumer spending across major food and beverage brands.

quiz.prompt: Why did PepsiCo miss earnings estimates despite strong international demand?
quiz.explanation: PepsiCo reported weaker-than-expected quarterly earnings because U.S. consumers are tightening their budgets, even as international demand stayed strong.

cards.headline: Meta scraps AI Instagram feature after privacy outcry
cards.summary: Meta launched a feature this week allowing users to generate AI images from public Instagram profile pictures, but pulled it within days following widespread criticism from privacy campaigners who called it a 'recipe for disaster.' The company said its intent was to provide a useful creative tool and that people could opt out, but acknowledged the feedback. Meta said it is discontinuing the feature because it 'misses the mark' on users' privacy.

quiz.prompt: Why did Meta pull its new AI image feature from Instagram?
options: It caused technical outages on the platform
Privacy campaigners said it was a 'recipe for disaster' and it missed the mark on privacy
Users found it too difficult to use
quiz.explanation: Meta said the feature missed the mark on users' privacy, and privacy campaigners had called it a 'recipe for disaster,' prompting the company to discontinue it.

cards.headline: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive design
cards.summary: EU regulators have accused Meta of breaching the Digital Services Act by failing to tackle mental health risks from addictive design features on Facebook and Instagram. Features such as infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and highly personalized recommendation algorithms are said to contribute to 'compulsive use' and 'unhealthy habits.' Meta now faces potential massive fines if it does not address the concerns.

quiz.prompt: Which EU law are regulators citing in their action against Meta's addictive features?
quiz.explanation: Regulators cited the Digital Services Act in threatening Meta with fines over features like infinite scroll and autoplay on Facebook and Instagram.

cards.headline: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
cards.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI on Friday alleging the AI company poached Apple workers and coaxed them to share confidential material in a bid to build a hardware business. Apple said in the suit that OpenAI's nascent hardware operation is 'rotten to its core,' and alleged the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership including a longtime former Apple employee. The case marks a significant legal clash between two of the biggest names in technology.

quiz.prompt: What does Apple allege OpenAI did to obtain confidential information?
options: Poached Apple workers and coaxed them to share confidential material
Purchased stolen data from a third party
Used publicly available Apple source code
quiz.explanation: Apple's lawsuit alleges OpenAI poached Apple employees and coaxed them to share confidential material in order to build its hardware business.

cards.headline: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in record US foreign IPO
cards.summary: South Korean chip maker SK Hynix set pricing for its US listing, raising $26.5 billion in what is described as the biggest foreign IPO in US history. The company, a supplier of advanced memory chips, has seen profits skyrocket thanks to the global race to build AI data centres. SK Hynix and Samsung are now being urged to build new semiconductor factories on US soil.

quiz.prompt: What has primarily driven SK Hynix's surging profits ahead of its US listing?
options: The global race to build AI data centres
quiz.explanation: SK Hynix, a supplier of advanced memory chips, has seen profits skyrocket due to the global race to build AI data centres.

cards.headline: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs in sweeping restructure
cards.summary: Microsoft has announced layoffs of 4,800 employees as part of a significant restructure, equating to about 2.1% of its total workforce. Xbox was particularly hard hit, with 1,600 immediate job losses reported in that division. The cuts signal a major shift in strategy for one of the world's largest technology companies.

quiz.prompt: Approximately what percentage of Microsoft's total workforce do the 4,800 layoffs represent?
quiz.explanation: Microsoft's 4,800 job cuts equate to approximately 2.1% of its total workforce, with 1,600 of those losses coming immediately from the Xbox division.

cards.headline: Parents warned as AI fuels child image abuse risk
cards.summary: The UK's National Crime Agency has warned that there is a growing threat of children's images being used to create child sexual abuse material through AI tools. Civil liberties and child safety groups say AI nudification apps are becoming more sophisticated and accessible to online predators. A separate class action lawsuit in the US has expanded against AI makers including Stability AI over deepfake child sexual abuse material.

quiz.prompt: Which UK agency warned parents about the risk of children's images being used by AI to create abuse material?
quiz.explanation: The UK's National Crime Agency warned that children's images are increasingly being exploited by AI tools to create child sexual abuse material.

cards.headline: Big tech's carbon emissions surge amid AI data centre boom
cards.summary: Microsoft, Amazon, and Google's collective carbon emissions have increased by nearly a fifth, now amounting to roughly a third of the carbon output of France, driven by a boom in data centre construction for AI. Despite the surge, all three companies say they still aim to achieve net zero emissions. The gap between stated climate goals and actual emissions trajectories is drawing renewed scrutiny.

quiz.prompt: By approximately how much have Microsoft, Amazon, and Google's collective carbon emissions increased?
quiz.explanation: According to the Guardian, the three companies' collective carbon emissions have increased by nearly a fifth, driven largely by the data centre construction boom for AI.

cards.headline: China recovers first reusable orbital rocket booster
cards.summary: China's state-owned space company successfully recovered its first orbital rocket booster after launch, marking a significant milestone in the country's space programme. Observers noted that China is clearly drawing inspiration from SpaceX's reusable rocket approach, with one commentator saying the country is 'trying to replicate' SpaceX's work. The development signals intensifying competition in the commercial and state space launch sector.

quiz.prompt: What achievement did China's state-owned space company reach with its rocket booster?
options: Recovery of its first orbital rocket booster after launch
Docking with the International Space Station
quiz.explanation: China's state-owned space company recovered its first orbital rocket booster after launch, a milestone observers linked to SpaceX's influence.

cards.headline: UK shops to alert police via real-time facial recognition
cards.summary: Facial recognition technology in UK shops including Sainsbury's and B&M will soon alert police in real time to the presence of people on watchlists, through a system called Facewatch. Civil liberties groups have called the rollout a 'dangerous escalation' of surveillance in everyday retail environments. The development raises fresh questions about privacy, consent, and the expansion of biometric identification in public spaces.

quiz.prompt: What is the name of the facial recognition system being rolled out in UK shops to alert police in real time?
quiz.explanation: The system is called Facewatch, and it will alert police in real time when people on watchlists are identified in participating shops like Sainsbury's and B&M.

cards.headline: Ransomware negotiator jailed for helping attackers
cards.summary: A Florida man hired to negotiate on behalf of ransomware victims was convicted after it emerged he was secretly working for the ransomware gang extorting those same victims, selling out the people he was paid to represent. He was sentenced to six years in prison. Prosecutors described him as having 'sold out the very victims he was hired to represent,' in a case highlighting a disturbing conflict of interest in the cybersecurity industry.

quiz.prompt: What was the sentence handed to the ransomware negotiator who betrayed his clients?
quiz.explanation: The man was sentenced to six years in prison after being convicted of working for the ransomware gang while purportedly representing its victims.

cards.headline: US strikes Iran; Hormuz closed, Gulf states hit
cards.summary: The United States carried out at least three rounds of strikes on Iran after an Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, which Tehran subsequently closed. Iran retaliated with missile and drone strikes targeting US military sites in Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan, Qatar, and the UAE, injuring at least three people in Qatar. A shaky ceasefire has since paused the fighting, though Iran's IRGC has vowed a 'crushing response' to any further US attacks.

quiz.prompt: What triggered the latest round of US strikes on Iran?
options: Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz
An Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz
Iran's refusal to attend ceasefire talks
quiz.explanation: The US strikes followed an Iranian attack on a vessel travelling through the Strait of Hormuz, according to multiple sources.

cards.headline: DOJ subpoenas NYT reporters over Air Force One coverage
cards.summary: The Justice Department subpoenaed several New York Times journalists, with federal agents appearing at their homes to compel grand jury testimony over their reporting on alleged security issues with President Trump's new Air Force One plane. The aircraft was gifted to Trump by Qatar. The Times confirmed the subpoenas, raising concerns about press freedom and the use of legal tools against reporters.

quiz.prompt: What was the New York Times reporting on that led to the subpoenas?
options: Alleged security issues with Trump's new Air Force One plane
quiz.explanation: The subpoenas were issued over reporters' coverage of alleged security issues with the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.

cards.headline: Senator Lindsey Graham dies after sudden illness
cards.summary: US Republican Senator Lindsey Graham, a close ally of President Trump and one of Israel's strongest supporters in the Senate, has died at the age of 71 following a brief and sudden illness. Graham had just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday. His death removes a prominent and influential voice from the US Senate.

quiz.prompt: Where had Lindsey Graham traveled just before his death?
quiz.explanation: Graham had just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, shortly before his sudden illness and death.

cards.headline: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad dies at 74
cards.summary: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the former Emir of Qatar who ruled from 1995 to 2013, has died aged 74. He is widely credited as the architect of modern Qatar, overseeing the country's transformation into a wealthy, globally prominent state. Sheikh Hamad stepped aside in 2013, handing power to his son Sheikh Tamim.

quiz.prompt: How long did Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani rule Qatar?
quiz.explanation: Sheikh Hamad ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013, an 18-year reign during which he oversaw the country's rise to global prominence.

cards.headline: China evacuates nearly two million amid typhoon Maysak
cards.summary: China evacuated nearly two million people as Typhoon Maysak, the first typhoon to make landfall in China this season, struck Zhejiang province. At least eleven people have been reported dead, with severe flooding leaving people stranded on rooftops and causing buildings to collapse. Hundreds of venomous snakes, including cobras, also escaped from flooded breeding farms in southern China during the disaster.

quiz.prompt: Which Chinese province was the primary focus of evacuations during Typhoon Maysak?
quiz.explanation: Schools, work, and outdoor activities were suspended in Zhejiang province as nearly two million people were evacuated ahead of the typhoon's landfall.

cards.headline: Israel now controls nearly 70% of Gaza
cards.summary: Nine months after a US-brokered ceasefire, Israel now controls nearly 70% of Gaza, up from about half when the ceasefire began. Israeli forces have continued pushing deeper into the territory, with Palestinians paying what reports describe as a deadly price. The EU is also facing accusations of dragging its feet on banning trade with illegal Israeli settlements, with foreign ministers set to discuss options.

quiz.prompt: Approximately what share of Gaza did Israel control when the ceasefire was first brokered?
quiz.explanation: When the US brokered the ceasefire, Israel controlled about half of Gaza; that figure has since grown to nearly 70%.

cards.headline: Largest housing affordability bill becomes law unsigned
cards.summary: The largest housing affordability bill in decades has become law in the United States without President Trump's signature. Trump refused to sign the legislation in protest over Congress failing to pass his sweeping voter ID bill. The unsigned bill's passage marks an unusual moment in which a significant piece of legislation was enacted against the sitting president's explicit objections.

quiz.prompt: Why did President Trump refuse to sign the housing affordability bill?
options: Congress did not pass his voter ID bill
quiz.explanation: Trump refused to sign the bill in protest over Congress not passing his sweeping voter ID legislation, though the bill became law regardless.

cards.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll surpasses 4,300
cards.summary: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, with nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing. President Delcy Rodríguez and the UN have called for financial help as the scale of the recovery effort looms large. Discontent with the Trump-backed government is mounting as authorities appear ill-prepared to respond to the disaster.

quiz.prompt: How many people have been reported injured in Venezuela's twin earthquakes?
quiz.explanation: According to reports, nearly 17,000 people were injured in Venezuela's twin earthquakes, with thousands more listed as missing.

cards.headline: US Democrat Ro Khanna detained by Israeli settlers
cards.summary: Democratic Congressman Ro Khanna, 49, says he was detained for 90 minutes by armed Israeli settlers while visiting the occupied West Bank. Khanna described the incident personally, highlighting the conditions faced by visitors and Palestinians in the territory. The episode drew attention to the situation on the ground in the West Bank amid ongoing conflict in the region.

quiz.prompt: How long does Ro Khanna say he was held by Israeli settlers in the West Bank?
quiz.explanation: Congressman Ro Khanna said he was detained by armed Israeli settlers for 90 minutes during his visit to the occupied West Bank.

cards.headline: Poor nations spend more on debt than education
cards.summary: A UNESCO report has revealed that most developing countries spent less on education last year than they did repaying foreign debt. Across 113 countries, debt repayments outpaced education spending, with 18 countries spending five times more on servicing loans than on schooling children. The findings have renewed calls for reform of international debt structures affecting low-income nations.

quiz.prompt: How many countries spent more on foreign debt repayment than on education, according to the UNESCO report?
quiz.explanation: The UNESCO report found that in 113 countries, debt repayments to foreign creditors outpaced spending on education for children.

cards.headline: Bellingham brace sends England to World Cup semis
cards.summary: Jude Bellingham scored twice, including an extra-time winner, as England came from behind to beat Norway 2-1 in Miami to reach the World Cup semi-finals. The victory was not without controversy — Norway argued Bellingham's equaliser should not have counted after claims the ball struck a Spidercam cable, though FIFA's ball sensor showed no evidence of contact. England manager Thomas Tuchel admitted his side were 'sloppy' and 'lucky,' while Bellingham responded dismissively with 'Yeah, well, whatever.'

quiz.prompt: What did FIFA's ball sensor data show regarding Norway's Spidercam cable claim?
options: The ball clearly struck the cable
There was no evidence of contact
quiz.explanation: FIFA said its ball sensor showed no evidence of the ball making contact with the Spidercam cable before England's equaliser.

cards.headline: Argentina stun Switzerland to face England
cards.summary: Argentina set up a blockbuster World Cup semi-final against England after beating 10-man Switzerland 3-1 in Kansas City, with Julián Álvarez scoring an extra-time stunner. Switzerland's Breel Embolo was sent off after becoming the first player dismissed under a new 'mistaken identity' VAR law at the World Cup. The semi-final will be the first meeting between Lionel Messi and England, and the first between the two nations since 2005.

quiz.prompt: Why was Switzerland's Breel Embolo sent off against Argentina?
options: Two bookable offences in normal time
quiz.explanation: Embolo became the first player to be sent off as a result of a new law for 'mistaken identity' at the World Cup.

cards.headline: McGregor's UFC return lasts 69 seconds with knee injury
cards.summary: Conor McGregor's long-awaited UFC return ended in the first round after just 69 seconds when he suffered a serious knee injury against Max Holloway at UFC 329 in Las Vegas. UFC chief Dana White said they were 'assuming a blown ACL,' and it was McGregor's first fight in five years. McGregor released a statement saying he was 'beyond dark' but denied entering the fight with a pre-existing injury.

quiz.prompt: How long did Conor McGregor's fight with Max Holloway last at UFC 329?
quiz.explanation: McGregor's return ended after just 69 seconds in the first round due to a knee injury.

cards.headline: Pimblett submits Saint-Denis in 52 seconds at UFC 329
cards.summary: Paddy Pimblett submitted Benoit Saint-Denis in just 52 seconds at UFC 329, dramatically strengthening his claim for a UFC lightweight title shot. After the win, Pimblett called out Conor McGregor, Ilia Topuria, and lightweight champion Justin Gaethje. The Liverpool fighter's explosive performance made him one of the standout stories of the night.

quiz.prompt: How quickly did Paddy Pimblett submit Benoit Saint-Denis at UFC 329?
quiz.explanation: Pimblett submitted Saint-Denis in just 52 seconds, the fastest finish of the main card.

cards.headline: Noskova wins maiden Wimbledon title in comeback thriller
cards.summary: Linda Noskova claimed her first Grand Slam title at Wimbledon, beating fellow Czech Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3, overcoming six championship points along the way. Muchova, who was appearing in her second major final, fell short in a dramatic contest. Noskova's victory made her the third Czech women's Wimbledon champion in four years.

quiz.prompt: Who did Linda Noskova defeat to win the Wimbledon women's title?
quiz.explanation: Noskova beat fellow Czech Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 to claim her maiden Grand Slam title.

cards.headline: Sinner wary of Zverev ahead of Wimbledon men's final
cards.summary: Wimbledon defending champion Jannik Sinner is preparing to face French Open champion Alexander Zverev in the men's final. Despite recent history suggesting Sinner should be confident, he expressed wariness about his opponent heading into the match. The final sets up a clash between two of the sport's current major champions.

quiz.prompt: What Grand Slam title does Alexander Zverev hold heading into the Wimbledon final?
quiz.explanation: Zverev is the reigning French Open champion as he faces Sinner in the Wimbledon men's final.

cards.headline: Seattle Seahawks sold for NFL-record $9.6 billion
cards.summary: The Seattle Seahawks are set to be sold for $9.6 billion, a record price in NFL history that smashes the previous record set just three years ago. The historic deal will see billions of dollars directed to charity as part of the sale terms. The transaction underscores the soaring valuations of major American sports franchises.

quiz.prompt: What is the reported sale price of the Seattle Seahawks?
quiz.explanation: The Seahawks are being sold for a reported $9.6 billion, an NFL-record price that surpasses the previous record set three years ago.

cards.headline: Klopp agrees deal to become Germany head coach
cards.summary: Jürgen Klopp has reached an agreement on key points to become the next Germany head coach, replacing Julian Nagelsmann. Germany exited the 2026 World Cup in the last 32, losing to Paraguay. Klopp had previously managed Liverpool before departing the club.

quiz.prompt: Which country did Germany lose to in the 2026 World Cup last 32?
quiz.explanation: Germany lost to Paraguay in the last 32 of the 2026 World Cup, which contributed to Nagelsmann's departure and Klopp's appointment.

cards.headline: South Africa World Cup midfielder Jayden Adams dies aged 25
cards.summary: Jayden Adams, who played in South Africa's three group games at the 2026 World Cup, has died at the age of 25. The midfielder played for Mamelodi Sundowns and had represented his country on the biggest stage just weeks before his death. No further details about the cause of death were provided in reports.

quiz.prompt: Which club did the late Jayden Adams play for?
quiz.explanation: Jayden Adams played for Mamelodi Sundowns and had featured in South Africa's three World Cup group games before his death at 25.

cards.headline: Ryu shoots LPGA major-record 60 to lead Evian Championship
cards.summary: South Korean golfer Haeran Ryu set an LPGA major scoring record with an 11-under 60 at the Evian Championship, opening up a three-shot lead going into the final round. The historic round marked Ryu's second major push in the space of just three weeks. The score is the lowest ever recorded in an LPGA major championship.

quiz.prompt: What score did Haeran Ryu shoot to set the LPGA major record at the Evian Championship?
quiz.explanation: Ryu shot an 11-under 60, the lowest score ever recorded in an LPGA major, to take a three-shot lead into the final round.

cards.headline: US strikes Iran as Strait of Hormuz closes
cards.summary: The United States launched multiple rounds of strikes on Iran after an Iranian attack on a Cyprus-flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran closed the critical waterway and retaliated with missile strikes targeting Gulf states including Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, the UAE, Jordan, and Oman. Three people were reported injured by falling shrapnel in Qatar, and Iran's IRGC vowed a crushing response to any further US attacks.

quiz.prompt: What triggered the latest round of US strikes on Iran?
options: Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz
An Iranian attack on a Cyprus-flagged vessel
Iran launching missiles at Gulf states
Iran targeting a US military base in Bahrain
quiz.explanation: The US strikes followed an Iranian attack on a Cyprus-flagged vessel travelling through the Strait of Hormuz.

cards.headline: Senator Lindsey Graham dies after sudden illness
cards.summary: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a Republican from South Carolina and close ally of Donald Trump, has died following a brief and sudden illness at age 71. Graham had recently returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky. He had served in the Senate since 2003 and was known for advocating a muscular US foreign policy.

quiz.prompt: Where had Lindsey Graham traveled shortly before his death?
quiz.explanation: Graham had just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky, before his sudden illness and death.

cards.headline: Former Qatar Emir Sheikh Hamad dies aged 74
cards.summary: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the former Emir of Qatar who ruled from 1995 to 2013, has died aged 74. He is widely credited as the architect of modern Qatar, overseeing the country's meteoric rise as an energy-rich Gulf power. Global leaders offered condolences and praised his legacy following the announcement.

quiz.prompt: How long did Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani rule Qatar?
quiz.explanation: Sheikh Hamad ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013, an 18-year reign during which he oversaw the country's dramatic rise.

cards.headline: China evacuates nearly two million amid powerful typhoon
cards.summary: China evacuated nearly two million people as a powerful typhoon made landfall, with the impact centred on Zhejiang province. Schools, workplaces, and outdoor activities were suspended, and many transport services were cancelled. The scale of the evacuation reflects the severity of the storm's threat to the region.

quiz.prompt: Which Chinese province was most affected by the powerful typhoon?
quiz.explanation: Schools, work, and outdoor activities were suspended in Zhejiang province as the typhoon made landfall.

cards.headline: Former UK MP Ann Widdecombe found dead at home
cards.summary: Former British MP Ann Widdecombe was found dead at her home in Haytor, Devon, prompting a murder investigation. A 28-year-old man was arrested in Rotherham, South Yorkshire, in connection with the case. Police said there is nothing to suggest the killing was politically motivated and they are not looking for anyone else.

quiz.prompt: What did police say about the motive in Ann Widdecombe's killing?
options: They found no evidence of a political motive
They believe it was financially motivated
They have not commented on motive
quiz.explanation: Police stated there is nothing to suggest the killing was politically motivated and they are not looking for anyone else after arresting a 28-year-old man.

cards.headline: Trump DOJ subpoenas New York Times journalists
cards.summary: The Trump administration has issued subpoenas to several New York Times journalists, ordering them to testify before a grand jury. The subpoenas follow the Times' reporting on security concerns related to the Qatar-gifted Air Force One plane. The move has raised significant press freedom concerns.

quiz.prompt: What reporting prompted the Trump administration's subpoenas against New York Times journalists?
options: Reporting on security concerns with the Qatar-gifted Air Force One
quiz.explanation: The subpoenas came after the Times reported on security concerns involving the new Air Force One gifted by Qatar.

cards.headline: 200 young campers rescued in Missouri-Kentucky flooding
cards.summary: A historic rainfall event caused widespread flooding across several Missouri counties and parts of Kentucky, leaving communities underwater. Around 200 young campers were rescued as part of emergency evacuations prompted by the severe flooding. Water rescues were ongoing as the scale of the disaster became clear.

quiz.prompt: Approximately how many young campers were rescued during the Missouri-Kentucky flooding?
quiz.explanation: According to NPR, around 200 young campers were rescued as flooding hit parts of Missouri and Kentucky during a historic rainfall event.

cards.headline: England and Argentina set up World Cup semi-final
cards.summary: England beat Norway 2-1 in extra time in Miami, with Jude Bellingham scoring both goals including a winner in extra time, to advance to the World Cup semi-finals. Argentina defeated Switzerland 3-1, with Julian Alvarez scoring in the 112th minute to seal the win. The two nations will now face each other in Wednesday's semi-final, reviving one of football's most storied rivalries.

quiz.prompt: Who scored the winning goal to send England into the World Cup semi-finals?
quiz.explanation: Jude Bellingham scored both of England's goals, including the extra-time winner, in the 2-1 victory over Norway.

cards.headline: British couple rescued with severe burns in Spain wildfires
cards.summary: A British couple were rescued from a ravine in Spain's Almeria province after being trapped by raging wildfires, reportedly found semi-conscious with burns covering 40% of their bodies. The incident comes as wildfire risk in parts of the UK is also described as exceptional, with heat health alerts issued during a prolonged heatwave. A Belgian man was separately reported to have died in the same Spanish wildfire, with his son disputing authorities' claims about warnings issued.

quiz.prompt: What injuries did the rescued British couple sustain in the Spain wildfires?
quiz.explanation: The British couple were found semi-conscious with burns covering 40% of their bodies after being trapped in a ravine in Almeria province.

cards.headline: US congressman says settlers detained him in West Bank
cards.summary: US Congressman Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers during a visit to the occupied West Bank. Khanna noted the settlers were armed with US-made weapons and that Israeli Defense Forces refused to intervene. The incident has drawn attention to the situation in the West Bank and prompted broader debate about US-Israel relations.

quiz.prompt: What did Ro Khanna say about the weapons carried by the settlers who detained him?
options: They were armed with Russian-made weapons
They were armed with US-made weapons
He did not comment on their weapons
quiz.explanation: Khanna specifically stated that the settlers were armed with US-made weapons and that Israeli Defense Forces refused to intervene.
